title: Coordinates, Graphs, Functions, and Rates of Change · Decision Defense
summary: Model water use, travel, cost, and capacity from tables, graphs, formulas, and descriptions. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a multi-representation resource dashboard. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Plot and interpret ordered pairs with axis quantities, units, origin, and scale.
Determine whether a relationship is a function and define practical input and output domains.
Translate a relationship among words, tables, graphs, and formulas while preserving meaning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.instruction: A model passes through (2, 8) and (6, 20). Calculate its average rate of change.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (20 − 8) / (6 − 2).

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (20 − 8) / (6 − 2).
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units per input or per input.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Coordinate Plane evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Coordinate Plane assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Intercept Domain applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Interpret intercepts and restrict conclusions to the meaningful domain of a model.

hints: Name the Intercept Domain precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

checks.description: The calculated quantity is accurate to the stated tolerance and carries its requested unit.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using interpret and intercepts plus specific case evidence.
